reasoning: Old Dominion Freight Line (ODFL) recently reported Q1 2025 earnings showing an 11.2% year-over-year decline in EPS and lowered its 2025 CapEx guidance due to market sluggishness. While the board increased the quarterly dividend by 7.7% to $0.28, the stock has experienced significant short-term negative downward momentum over the past week, dropping from $170.4 on May 19 down to $160.4 on May 23. Given this bearish momentum in the immediate short term, the probability of it recovering and closing higher than $160.44 by June 1 (just 5 trading days away) is relatively low, and thus anchored slightly below 0.5. Following the guideline that weekly stock moves are largely unpredictable but uncertainty must increase with the forecast horizon (probabilities approaching 0.5 for distant dates), the short-term negative signal fades into a coin-flip. Therefore, estimates begin below 0.5 and slowly regress toward 0.5 for the 6-month horizon.
